title: A Smaller Version of LIME
text: goal kumquat smaller simpler implementation LIME. purely demonstration purposes, ideal used production settings. Kumquat super easy use. First get data set model set . decide data points interest kumquat give list information point selected. go step--step guide setting kumquats used.

title: A Smaller Version of LIME
text: can install development version kumquat like : can install CRAN release kumquat running:
code: pak::pak("janithwanni/kumquat") install.packages("kumquat")

title: A Smaller Version of LIME
code: library(kumquat)

title: A Smaller Version of LIME
text: Currently kumquat supports datasets two numeric variables one categorical variable.

title: A Smaller Version of LIME
code: library(tidyverse) #> ── Attaching core tidyverse packages ──────────────────────── tidyverse 2.0.0 ── #> ✔ dplyr     1.2.1     ✔ readr     2.2.0 #> ✔ forcats   1.0.1     ✔ stringr   1.6.0 #> ✔ ggplot2   4.0.3     ✔ tibble    3.3.1 #> ✔ lubridate 1.9.5     ✔ tidyr     1.3.2 #> ✔ purrr     1.2.2      #> ── Conflicts ────────────────────────────────────────── tidyverse_conflicts() ── #> ✖ dplyr::filter() masks stats::filter() #> ✖ dplyr::lag()    masks stats::lag() #> ℹ Use the conflicted package (<http://conflicted.r-lib.org/>) to force all conflicts to become errors library(colorspace)  data(d_vertical)  ggplot(d_vertical, aes(x = x, y = y, colour = class)) +   geom_point() +   scale_colour_discrete_divergingx(palette = "Zissou 1") +   theme_minimal() +   theme(aspect.ratio = 1)

title: A Smaller Version of LIME
text: setting model, kumquat expects bundle object containing model reference pointers.
code: library(randomForest) #> randomForest 4.7-1.2 #> Type rfNews() to see new features/changes/bug fixes. #>  #> Attaching package: 'randomForest' #> The following object is masked from 'package:dplyr': #>  #>     combine #> The following object is masked from 'package:ggplot2': #>  #>     margin library(bundle)  # Get model ready rfmodel <- randomForest(   class ~ x + y,   data = d_vertical )  # Bundle model up rfmodel_bundled <- bundle(rfmodel)

what: Step 3: Decide on points of interest
title: A Smaller Version of LIME
code: # Decide on points of interest find_closest <- function(pt, data) {   dst <- data |>     mutate(dst = sqrt((x - pt$x)^2 + (y - pt$y)^2))   return(which.min(dst$dst)) }  pois <- c(   # Case 1: the point of interest is not near the boundary   find_closest(tibble(x=0, y=0), d_vertical),   # Case 2: the point is on the decision boundary   find_closest(tibble(x=0.3, y=0.5), d_vertical) ) ggplot(d_vertical, aes(x = x, y = y, colour = class)) +   geom_point() +   geom_point(data=d_vertical[pois, ], mapping=aes(x=x,y=y,fill=class), shape = 18, color = "black") +   scale_colour_discrete_divergingx(palette = "Zissou 1") +   theme_minimal() +   theme(aspect.ratio = 1)

previous_headings: Usage > Step 3: Decide on points of interest
title: A Smaller Version of LIME
code: # Run kumquat ks <- kumquat(   rfmodel_bundled,   d_vertical,   pois,   class_names = unique(d_vertical$class) )

previous_headings: Usage > Step 3: Decide on points of interest > Step 5: Examine the outputs
what: Case 1: The point is not near the decision boundary
title: A Smaller Version of LIME
text: case, according ks[[1]]$local_model$importances x y equally important.

previous_headings: Usage > Step 3: Decide on points of interest > Step 5: Examine the outputs
what: Case 2: The point is near the decision boundary
title: A Smaller Version of LIME
text: case, according ks[[2]]$local_model$importances, x importance -1000.3018471 y importance 0. Since decision boundary made using just x variable expect x variable important model’s decision making process. output kumquat list containing following elements. perturbations: data.frame perturbations used fit local model local_model: Details glmnet model fit. also list containing following elements. case point interest near model’s decision boundary, model component NULL importances distributted equally. glm_predictions importances: importances feature coef_mat: coefficients model: glm_net model object point_of_interest train_data

title: Complex Boundary Example Dataset — d_multi
text: demonstration dataset containing two numeric predictors binary class variable. class boundary set x < (-0.5) & y < (-0.3) x >= -0.5 & x < 0.4 & y < 0.1 + 0.8 * x x >= 0.4 B elsewhere

title: Complex Boundary Example Dataset — d_multi
code: data(d_multi)

title: Complex Boundary Example Dataset — d_multi
text: tibble 5,000 rows 3 variables: x Numeric independent variable generated uniform distribution [-1, 1] y Numeric independent variable generated uniform distribution [-1, 1] class Binary categorical variable two levels: "" "B"

title: Complex Boundary Example Dataset — d_multi
text: Simple two-dimensional dataset complex decison boundary.

what: Fit Local Linear Model and Compute Feature Importance — fit_local_model
title: Fit Local Linear Model and Compute Feature Importance — fit_local_model
text: Fit Local Linear Model Compute Feature Importance

title: Fit Local Linear Model and Compute Feature Importance — fit_local_model
code: fit_local_model(   perturbations,   predictor_vars,   nfolds = 50,   alpha = 1,   class_names = c("A", "B") )

title: Fit Local Linear Model and Compute Feature Importance — fit_local_model
text: perturbations Data frame perturbations predictions predictor_vars Character vector predictor variable names nfolds Number folds cross-validation (default: 50) alpha Elastic net mixing parameter (default: 1 lasso) class_names Character vector class names binary classification

title: Fit Local Linear Model and Compute Feature Importance — fit_local_model
text: list containing glm_predictions, importances, fitted model

title: Fit Local Linear Model and Compute Feature Importance — fit_local_model
code: perturbations <- data.frame(    x1 = c(1, 2, 3),    x2 = c(4, 5, 6),    pred = c("A", "A", "A")  )   result <- fit_local_model(    perturbations,    predictor_vars = c("x1", "x2")  )

what: Generate Perturbations Around a Point of Interest — generate_perturbations
title: Generate Perturbations Around a Point of Interest — generate_perturbations
text: Generate Perturbations Around Point Interest

title: Generate Perturbations Around a Point of Interest — generate_perturbations
code: generate_perturbations(   data,   poi,   radius = 0.1,   step = 0.01,   predictors = names(data) )

title: Generate Perturbations Around a Point of Interest — generate_perturbations
text: data Training data frame poi Row number point interest radius Perturbation radius (default: 0.1) step Step size perturbations (default: 0.01) predictors Character vector predictor variable names

title: Generate Perturbations Around a Point of Interest — generate_perturbations
text: data frame perturbed points. output contains following properties: Columns predictors Number rows going dependent radius step

title: Generate Perturbations Around a Point of Interest — generate_perturbations
code: data <- data.frame(   x = 1,   y = 2 ) result <- generate_perturbations(   data,   poi = 1,   radius = 0.1,   step = 0.1,   predictors = c("x", "y")  )

title: Complete Local Interpretation Pipeline — kumquat
text: Complete Local Interpretation Pipeline

title: Complete Local Interpretation Pipeline — kumquat
code: kumquat(   model_bundle,   data,   pois,   perturbations = NULL,   radius = 0.1,   step = 0.01,   predictor_vars = c("x", "y"),   nfolds = 50,   alpha = 1,   class_names = c("A", "B"),   predict_func = stats::predict )

title: Complete Local Interpretation Pipeline — kumquat
text: model_bundle trained model held bundle::bundle() data Training data pois Points interest (row numbers) perturbations data.frame perturbations used fit local model radius Perturbation radius (default: 0.1) step Perturbation step size (default: 0.01) predictor_vars Character vector predictor variable names nfolds Number CV folds (default: 50) alpha Elastic net parameter (default: 1) class_names Character vector class names predict_func function takes two arguments: model data returns vector factors

title: Complete Local Interpretation Pipeline — kumquat
text: list containing perturbations, predictions, local model results

title: Complete Local Interpretation Pipeline — kumquat
code: data(d_vertical) rfmodel <- randomForest::randomForest(  class ~ x + y,  data = d_vertical ) # Bundle model up rfmodel_bundled <- bundle::bundle(rfmodel) ks <- kumquat(  rfmodel_bundled,  d_vertical,   1,   class_names = unique(d_vertical$class) ) #> INFO [2026-06-04 03:53:22] Picking kumquats for row: 1

what: Extract and Combine Feature Importances from Kumquat Objects — pinch_importance
title: Extract and Combine Feature Importances from Kumquat Objects — pinch_importance
text: pinch_importance() extracts feature importance values kumquats combines single data frame.

title: Extract and Combine Feature Importances from Kumquat Objects — pinch_importance
code: pinch_importance(kumquats)

title: Extract and Combine Feature Importances from Kumquat Objects — pinch_importance
text: kumquats result call make_kumquats

title: Extract and Combine Feature Importances from Kumquat Objects — pinch_importance
text: data.frame row corresponds feature importances extracted one kumquat object. Column names represent feature names.

title: Extract and Combine Feature Importances from Kumquat Objects — pinch_importance
code: data(d_vertical) rfmodel <- randomForest::randomForest(  class ~ x + y,  data = d_vertical ) # Bundle model up rfmodel_bundled <- bundle::bundle(rfmodel) ks <- kumquat(  rfmodel_bundled,  d_vertical,   1,   class_names = unique(d_vertical$class) ) #> INFO [2026-06-04 03:53:23] Picking kumquats for row: 1 imps <- pinch_importance(ks)

what: Plot point of interest with perturbations and predictions — plot_interest
title: Plot point of interest with perturbations and predictions — plot_interest
text: function generates ggplot visualizing perturbations local model, overlaying training data highlighting specific point interest (poi). plot subtitle shows importances first two features.

title: Plot point of interest with perturbations and predictions — plot_interest
code: plot_interest(kquat)

title: Plot point of interest with perturbations and predictions — plot_interest
text: kquat list-like object containing least: local_model$importances numeric vector local_model$glm_predictions numeric vector perturbations data.frame columns x y train_data data.frame columns x, y, class poi integer index point interest

title: Plot point of interest with perturbations and predictions — plot_interest
text: ggplot object

title: Plot point of interest with perturbations and predictions — plot_interest
code: data(d_vertical) rfmodel <- randomForest::randomForest(  class ~ x + y,  data = d_vertical ) # Bundle model up rfmodel_bundled <- bundle::bundle(rfmodel) ks <- kumquat(  rfmodel_bundled,  d_vertical,   1,   class_names = unique(d_vertical$class) ) #> INFO [2026-06-04 03:53:24] Picking kumquats for row: 1 plot_obj <- plot_interest(ks)
